result:
# Introducing Smart Drafts

We've all been there: you open a new document, type the title, and then the cursor blinks back at you for a while. That blank page is one of the most common places our users tell us they get stuck, and it's the reason we built Smart Drafts. Starting today, every new document can begin with a thoughtful, AI-suggested outline — so the hardest part of writing, getting started, happens in a single click.

Smart Drafts works quietly in the background of the editor you already know. When you create a new document, just click **Start with an outline** in the toolbar. We'll read the document title and look at the handful of recent documents you've been working on to understand the context you're writing in, then propose a structured outline tailored to that shape of work — whether it's a project brief, a retro, a product spec, or a weekly update. Everything it suggests is a starting point, not a constraint: edit freely, rearrange sections, or dismiss the draft entirely and write your own way. Nothing is shared outside your workspace, and outlines are generated only when you ask for one.

This is the first step in a broader push to make the editor feel more like a thoughtful collaborator and less like an empty canvas. Next up, we're working on **custom voice training**, which will let Smart Drafts learn the rhythm and tone of how *you* write, so the outlines — and eventually the drafts themselves — sound unmistakably like your team. We'll have more to share on that in the coming weeks. In the meantime, open a new document and give Smart Drafts a try. We'd love to hear what you think.

# Introducing Smart Drafts

We've all been there: you open a new document, type the title, and then the cursor blinks back at you for a while. That blank page is one of the most common places our users tell us they get stuck, and it's the reason we built Smart Drafts. Starting today, every new document can begin with a thoughtful, AI-suggested outline — so the hardest part of writing, getting started, happens in a single click.

Smart Drafts works quietly in the background of the editor you already know. When you create a new document, just click **Start with an outline** in the toolbar. We'll read the document title and look at the handful of recent documents you've been working on to understand the context you're writing in, then propose a structured outline tailored to that shape of work — whether it's a project brief, a retro, a product spec, or a weekly update. Everything it suggests is a starting point, not a constraint: edit freely, rearrange sections, or dismiss the draft entirely and write your own way. Nothing is shared outside your workspace, and outlines are generated only when you ask for one.

This is the first step in a broader push to make the editor feel more like a thoughtful collaborator and less like an empty canvas. Next up, we're working on **custom voice training**, which will let Smart Drafts learn the rhythm and tone of how *you* write, so the outlines — and eventually the drafts themselves — sound unmistakably like your team. We'll have more to share on that in the coming weeks. In the meantime, open a new document and give Smart Drafts a try. We'd love to hear what you think.
